factual-accuracy.requirement: Calculates IOS Q2 2025 margin as 24.7% using $166.7M profit / $675.7M sales (within +/- 0.2pp)

factual-accuracy.requirement: Calculates IOS FY2024 margin as 26.0% using $704.6M profit / $2,714.7M sales (within +/- 0.2pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not calculate IOS FY2024 margin using the specified $704.6M profit and $2,714.7M sales, nor does it state a resulting margin of ~26.0%. Instead, it gives an estimated IOS 2024 margin of 24.7% based on different revenue/profit figures.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States IOS revenue growth 2023-2024 as +3.9% from $2,612.2M to $2,714.7M (within +/- 0.3pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not state IOS revenue growth from 2023 to 2024 as +3.9% from $2,612.2M to $2,714.7M. Instead, it uses different IOS revenue figures ($2,500M to $2,540M) and reports IOS growth as ~1.6%, so the required values are absent.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Calculates PT Q2 2025 margin as 17.8% using $93.2M profit / $523.6M sales (within +/- 0.2pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response calculates PT’s margin as 17.8% using the stated Q2 2025 figures of $93.2M operating profit and $523.6M sales (shown as “$523.6 × 17.8% = $93.2”), which is consistent with 93.2/523.6 ≈ 17.8% within the allowed tolerance.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Calculates PT FY2024 margin as 22.4% using $500.0M profit / $2,229.4M sales (within +/- 0.2pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not calculate PT FY2024 margin as 22.4% using $500.0M profit divided by $2,229.4M sales. Instead, it cites PT 2024 revenue as $2,226M with a 22.1% margin and does not present the required $500.0M/$2,229.4M computation or a 22.4% result.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States PT separation completed June 28, 2025 or began Q3 2025 as discontinued operation
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states that the Precision Technologies spin-off was completed on June 28, 2025 (e.g., in the Executive Summary and again in the Q2 2025 section). This satisfies the criterion’s requirement to state PT separation completed June 28, 2025.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States PT revenue growth 2023-2024 as +0.3% from $2,223.7M to $2,229.4M (within +/- 0.3pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response gives PT 2024 revenue as $2,226M but does not state the required 2023 PT revenue of $2,223.7M, the 2024 PT revenue of $2,229.4M, or the specific +0.3% growth rate between those two figures. Instead, it uses an estimated 2023 PT revenue (~$2,150M) and calculates ~3.6% growth.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Notes PT H1 2025 margins fell to 17.6% vs 23.8% prior year (within +/- 0.5pp for either figure)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response reports PT Q1 2025 operating margin as 17.8% and Q1 2024 as 20.9%, but it does not state PT H1 2025 margin of 17.6% nor the prior-year figure of 23.8%. It also does not provide an H1 comparison at all, so the required 17.6% vs 23.8% statement is missing.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States PT segment FY2024 capex as $34.3M or $34M-35M annually (within +/- $1M)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response discusses overall and segment capex trends (e.g., total capex ~$105M and AHS capex up ~46%) but does not state Precision Technologies (PT) FY2024 capex as $34.3M or within a $34M–$35M range. No PT-specific FY2024 capex figure appears anywhere in the response.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Notes PT driven Q2 2025 core decline in Western Europe with softness in China and North America
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response mentions PT had “near-term softness” and higher China exposure, but it does not specifically note a Q2 2025 core decline in Western Europe nor explicitly tie that decline to softness in China and North America. Western Europe is only referenced generally, without a Q2 2025 core decline attribution.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Calculates AHS Q2 2025 margin as 12.2% using $39.1M profit / $319.5M sales (within +/- 0.2pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not calculate AHS Q2 2025 margin as 12.2% using $39.1M profit divided by $319.5M sales. Instead, it reports AHS Q2 2025 revenue as $320M and an operating margin of 11.2% (with operating income ≈ $35.8M), so the specified 12.2% calculation is absent.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Calculates AHS FY2024 margin as 12.1% using $155.6M profit / $1,287.7M sales (within +/- 0.2pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not calculate AHS FY2024 margin using $155.6M profit divided by $1,287.7M sales, nor does it report a resulting margin near 12.1%. Instead, it gives an estimated AHS FY2024 margin of 25.0% based on different (estimated) revenue and operating income figures.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States AHS revenue growth 2023-2024 as +4.7% from $1,229.4M to $1,287.7M (within +/- 0.3pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not state AHS revenue growth from 2023 to 2024 as +4.7% nor does it cite the specific values $1,229.4M and $1,287.7M. Instead, it uses approximate AHS revenues ($1,200M to $1,300M) and calculates growth as 8.3%, which does not meet the required figures or tolerance.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Calculates combined IOS+AHS FY2024 margin as 21.5% using $860.2M profit / $4,002.4M sales (within +/- 0.2pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response never calculates the combined IOS+AHS FY2024 margin using $860.2M profit divided by $4,002.4M sales, nor does it state the resulting ~21.5% figure. It provides separate estimated segment margins and revenues, but not the required combined calculation with those exact inputs.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Calculates combined IOS+AHS Q2 2025 margin as 20.7% using $205.8M profit / $995.2M sales (within +/- 0.2pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not calculate a combined IOS+AHS Q2 2025 margin using $205.8M profit divided by $995.2M sales, nor does it report the resulting ~20.7% figure. It instead provides separate Q2 2025 margins for IOS (24.5%) and AHS (11.2%) with different revenue totals, so the specified combined calculation is missing.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Q1 2025 GAAP operating margin as 15.8% or adjusted operating margin as 25.3% (within +/- 0.3pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response reports Q1 2025 segment operating margins (e.g., IOS 25.3%) but does not state Fortive’s overall Q1 2025 GAAP operating margin as 15.8% or an overall adjusted operating margin as 25.3%. The only 25.3% figure shown is for the IOS segment, not company-wide.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Q1 2024 GAAP operating margin as 19.8% or adjusted operating margin as 25.1% (within +/- 0.3pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response provides segment operating margins and other metrics, but it does not state Fortive’s Q1 2024 GAAP operating margin as 19.8% nor an adjusted operating margin as 25.1% (±0.3pp). It only lists segment-level margins for Q1 2024 and does not give the overall company GAAP/adjusted operating margin figures required.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Q2 2025 favorable pricing contribution included tariff-related countermeasures and surcharges
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response discusses tariff headwinds and mentions pricing actions and mitigation, but it does not state that Q2 2025 favorable pricing contribution specifically included tariff-related countermeasures and surcharges. The required linkage between favorable pricing contribution and those tariff-related items is not explicitly made.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States H1 2025 separation costs as $64M in Unallocated Corporate Costs (within +/- $2M)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not state H1 2025 separation costs as $64M in Unallocated Corporate Costs. It only mentions “Restructuring and spin-off related costs totaled $3.9M in Q1 2025” and provides no H1 2025 separation-cost figure or Unallocated Corporate Costs breakout.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Explicitly identifies IOS as the segment with strongest unit economics citing its 24.7% Q2 2025 margin vs PT 17.8% and AHS 12.2%
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not explicitly identify IOS as having the strongest unit economics using the specified Q2 2025 margins of 24.7% (IOS) vs 17.8% (PT) and 12.2% (AHS). It instead lists Q2 2025 IOS margin as 24.5% and AHS as 11.2%, and does not make the required explicit comparison statement.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Q2 2025 vs FY2024 margin change for each segment (IOS -1.3pp, PT -4.6pp, AHS +0.1pp within +/-0.3pp)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not state the Q2 2025 vs FY2024 operating margin change for each segment with the specified values (IOS -1.3pp, PT -4.6pp, AHS +0.1pp within +/-0.3pp). It provides Q2 2025 margins for IOS and AHS and FY2024 margins, but it never computes or reports the required Q2 2025 vs FY2024 pp deltas for all three segments, and PT Q2 2025 margin is not given.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Q1 2025 vs Q1 2024 GAAP margin declined ~400bps due to prior-year $63M PT property gain, or adjusted margin improved +20bps
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response compares Q1 2024 vs Q1 2025 segment margins but does not state that the overall Q1 2025 vs Q1 2024 GAAP margin declined ~400 bps due to a prior-year $63M PT property gain, nor that adjusted margin improved by +20 bps. The specific $63M gain and the ~400 bps / +20 bps figures are absent.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Fortive prioritizes higher-margin IOS and/or growth-oriented AHS segments post-separation
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly says Fortive post-spin focuses on IOS and AHS and frames this as a deliberate prioritization of higher-margin, recurring-revenue businesses, describing IOS as software-enabled workflows and AHS as essential healthcare solutions.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Assigns explicit strategic roles (profitability anchor, cash generator, growth engine) to at least 2 segments
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response discusses segment positioning qualitatively (e.g., IOS and AHS as higher-margin, recurring-revenue focuses) but does not assign explicit strategic roles like “profitability anchor,” “cash generator,” or “growth engine” to any segments. None of those role labels are used for at least two segments.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: States coherent investment narrative (fund and grow, cash cow + growth kicker) showing IOS/AHS complementarity
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response describes Fortive’s post-spin focus on IOS and AHS, emphasizing higher-margin, recurring revenue and reduced cyclicality, and it frames IOS as software-enabled industrial workflows while AHS serves essential healthcare workflows. This provides a coherent portfolio narrative showing how IOS and AHS complement each other as the remaining core businesses.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Explains how PT divestiture improves portfolio composition, margin mix, or quality of remaining business
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response explicitly states that divesting PT removes a more cyclical, capital-intensive business and shifts Fortive toward higher-margin, recurring-revenue IOS and AHS, improving margin stability and reducing geographic (China) exposure. It also notes improved portfolio quality via greater managerial focus and capital allocation to the remaining segments.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Identifies reduced exposure to underperforming regions (Europe/China/North America) as strategic separation benefit
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response explicitly notes that the spin-off reduced Fortive’s China exposure and that PT previously had higher Asia/China exposure, framing this as a mitigated regional risk and portfolio-quality benefit. It also discusses moderated geographic risks post-spin, implying reduced exposure to underperforming regions as a strategic separation benefit.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Connects retained segments to mission-critical, essential, or specialized workflows in respective end markets
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response explicitly links IOS to mission-critical industrial automation/safety workflows and AHS to essential sterilization and infection control workflows in healthcare, describing how these support resilient demand and pricing power.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Analyzes through 4+ distinct lenses (unit economics, trajectory, capital allocation, geography, competitive positioning)
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response analyzes Fortive through multiple distinct lenses, including unit economics (segment operating margins), trajectory (YoY growth and margin changes), capital allocation (CapEx, buybacks, debt reduction), geography (regional revenue exposure/China), and competitive positioning (cyclicality reduction, recurring revenue, workflow essentiality).

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Provides holistic conclusion integrating segment metrics, separation rationale, and post-separation positioning
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response ends with a conclusions section that ties together segment margins and growth, explains the strategic rationale for spinning off Precision Technologies, and discusses how the remaining IOS and AHS businesses are positioned post-separation (recurring revenue, reduced cyclicality, competitive focus).

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Labels segment performance as "strong", "weak", or "promising" without comparison to prior periods, benchmarks, or other segments' figures
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response uses qualitative labels like “robust,” “resilient,” “stable,” and “strong” (e.g., “Segment profitability remains robust post-spin,” “IOS…resilient,” “AHS…stable”) without consistently tying those labels to explicit comparisons versus prior periods, benchmarks, or other segments’ figures.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses precise financial terms (operating margin, unit economics, operating leverage, mix quality)
presentation-quality.explanation: The response repeatedly uses precise financial terms such as operating margin and operating leverage, and it explicitly discusses unit economics and revenue mix quality (e.g., recurring revenue mix, higher-margin mix). These terms are used in context of segment performance and portfolio strategy.

presentation-quality.requirement: Organizes segment metrics with clear structure (tables, bullets, or systematic comparison format)
presentation-quality.explanation: The response organizes segment metrics using multiple clearly labeled tables (e.g., full-year 2023/2024, Q1 2024/2025, Q2 2025, and growth/margin change tables), providing a systematic comparison format.

presentation-quality.requirement: Shows calculation methodology for at least 3 margin computations with numerator/denominator
presentation-quality.explanation: The response shows multiple explicit margin calculation methods with numerator/denominator form, e.g., IOS Q2 2025 ($697 × 24.5% = $170.8), AHS Q2 2025 ($320 × 11.2% = $35.8), and PT Q1 2025 ($523.6 × 17.8% = $93.2), which each imply operating margin = operating income ÷ revenue.

presentation-quality.requirement: Maintains objective analytical tone without promotional language or unsupported opinions
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses an analytical, data-focused tone with calculations, comparisons, and sourcing, and it avoids overtly promotional phrasing or subjective hype. Strategic conclusions (e.g., reduced cyclicality, improved focus) are generally framed as implications of cited metrics rather than unsupported opinions.

presentation-quality.requirement: Restates the same strategic conclusion about the separation multiple times without adding new supporting data
presentation-quality.explanation: The response repeats the same strategic conclusion that spinning off PT improves portfolio quality by shedding a cyclical, capital-intensive business and sharpening focus on higher-margin/recurring IOS and AHS. This conclusion appears in the Executive Summary, Section 3.1, Section 3.6, and the final Conclusions without materially new supporting data each time.

presentation-quality.requirement: Presents segment financials as flowing paragraphs rather than structured tables, bullets, or comparison formats
presentation-quality.explanation: The response presents segment financials primarily in structured tables with rows/columns for revenue, margins, and operating income, plus bullet points and section headings. It does not present the segment financials as flowing paragraphs.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Fortive 10-K for fiscal year 2024 filed February 25, 2025 as a source
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites “Fortive 2024 Annual Report (10-K)” and provides a link whose accession number indicates a 2025 filing (0001659166-25-000024), corresponding to the FY2024 10-K filed on February 25, 2025.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Fortive 10-Q for Q1 2025 filed May 1, 2025 as a source
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites “Fortive 10-Q Q1 2025” as a source, but it does not state that this 10-Q was filed on May 1, 2025. The required filing date attribution (“filed May 1, 2025”) is missing from the citation.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Fortive 10-Q for Q2 2025 filed July 30, 2025 as a source
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites “Fortive 10-Q Q2 2025” as a source, but it does not state that this 10-Q was filed on July 30, 2025. The required filing date attribution (“filed July 30, 2025”) is missing from the citation.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites non-authoritative sources such as social media, blogs, news articles, or analyst opinions as primary data sources
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites non-authoritative sources as primary inputs, including Fiscal.ai for capex/tariff commentary and The Motley Fool for an earnings call transcript in the Sources section ([11] and [12]). These are used to support key claims rather than being treated as secondary or avoided.
